activities_projects.title: Ecosystem Model Design
activities_projects.objective: Design and explain a working ecosystem model
steps: Create a small closed ecosystem model using soil, plants, and water
Observe interactions between biotic and abiotic components for several days
Record changes in moisture, plant growth, and organisms present
Explain how the components maintain balance in the system
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand ecosystem balance and interdependence

activities_projects.title: Food Web Impact Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Analyse ecological consequences of species removal
steps: Construct a food web for a forest ecosystem with at least eight organisms
Select one species and assume it disappears
Predict how this will affect other organisms in the food web
Explain the cascading ecological effects
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop analytical skills in ecological systems
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of reasoning and accuracy (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Population Change Simulation
activities_projects.objective: Study population fluctuations in an ecosystem
steps: Create a simple ecosystem model with producers, herbivores, and carnivores
Assume population numbers for each organism
Simulate changes such as drought or predator removal
Calculate and explain population changes over time
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand ecological balance and population dynamics
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical reasoning and calculation accuracy (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Energy Flow Investigation
activities_projects.objective: Analyse energy transfer through trophic levels
steps: Select a food chain with at least four trophic levels
Estimate the energy available at each level using the 10% energy transfer rule
Create a pyramid of energy diagram
Explain why higher trophic levels support fewer organisms
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand energy flow and ecological pyramids
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of calculations and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Decomposition Rate Experiment
steps: Place equal amounts of dead leaves in two containers
Keep one container moist and the other dry
Observe decomposition for several days
Compare and explain the differences in decomposition rates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand environmental factors influencing decomposition

activities_projects.title: Ecosystem Interaction Mapping
activities_projects.objective: Map complex interactions among ecosystem components
steps: Choose a natural ecosystem such as a pond or forest
Identify at least ten organisms and environmental factors
Draw arrows showing interactions among them
Explain positive and negative interactions
activities_projects.evaluation: Completeness and clarity of interaction map (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Human Impact Case Study
steps: Research an ecosystem affected by human activities such as deforestation or pollution
Describe the original ecosystem conditions
Explain how human activities changed the ecosystem
Suggest sustainable solutions
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of research and proposed solutions (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Biodiversity Assessment
activities_projects.objective: Measure biodiversity in a small ecosystem
steps: Select a small ecosystem such as a garden or park
List all visible plant and animal species
Calculate species diversity based on the number of species present
Discuss why biodiversity is important for ecosystem stability
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand biodiversity and ecosystem resilience

activities_projects.title: Sustainable Agriculture Proposal
steps: Study current farming practices in your region
Identify environmental problems caused by chemical fertilizers or pesticides
Design a sustainable farming plan using organic practices
Explain how it benefits soil health and biodiversity
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students connect agriculture with ecosystem sustainability
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and feasibility of proposal (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Ecosystem Conservation Campaign
steps: Research environmental threats to ecosystems
Create posters or presentations explaining ecosystem importance
Suggest practical conservation steps for communities
Present the campaign in school
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop environmental awareness and advocacy skills
